summary: Conduct a compliance audit from framework mapping through gap identification to remediation planning.

inputs.name: compliance_framework
inputs.description: The regulatory or standards framework to audit against.

inputs.name: evidence_repository
inputs.description: Location of audit evidence and documentation.

outputs.name: compliance_matrix
outputs.description: Framework requirements mapped to current controls.

outputs.name: gap_analysis
outputs.description: Identified gaps between requirements and implementation.

outputs.name: remediation_plan
outputs.description: Prioritized plan for addressing identified gaps.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Keep issue spotting and human review explicit.

gates.rule: Confirm the task is compliance auditing and not certification or legal opinion.
Require framework identification and control inventory.
Distinguish between observed gaps and recommended remediation.
End with remediation plan for compliance-owner review; do not claim certification.

steps.action: Map framework requirements to control inventory.
Identify gaps between requirements and current implementation.
Provide regulatory context and upcoming changes affecting gaps.
Prioritize gaps by risk exposure and remediation effort.
Compile remediation plan with timelines and ownership.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read documents, policies, and reference materials.

verification.method: Check the output names the audited framework and scope.
Check each gap includes requirement, current state, and severity.
Check output includes prioritized remediation with ownership.

success_criteria: Framework requirements are fully mapped to controls.
Each gap includes severity, rationale, and suggested remediation.
Output ends with remediation plan for compliance-owner review.

rollback.when: If scope, confidentiality, or review authority is unclear, revert the draft and regenerate a narrower output for counsel review.
